title: AI-Powered CDN (Predictive Prefetch)
subtitle: The CDN that knows what you'll click next

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: A regular CDN waits for you to order coffee before pouring. An AI-powered CDN watches you walk in, recognises you're a latte person, and starts steaming the milk before you reach the counter.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Akamai's Predictive Prefetch (part of Ion) uses ML models trained on real navigation patterns to predict which assets a user will need next. The CDN pushes resources to the browser cache before the user clicks.

- Models trained on anonymised mPulse RUM data
- Per-page prediction: which links/images are likely next
- No origin changes required — config in Property Manager
- Pairs with HTTP/2 Server Push and Resource Hints (preload, prefetch)

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: E-commerce product listing → product detail page transition: Predictive Prefetch pre-loads hero images and JS bundles for the top-3 most-likely-clicked products. Result: perceived page load drops by 30-50% for predicted navigations. Works best on high-traffic sites where the model has strong signal.
"Predictive Prefetch = ML-driven pre-loading. The CDN anticipates what users need next, cutting perceived load time by up to 50%."

quiz.question: What data does Predictive Prefetch use to train its models?

options.explain: Server logs miss client-side navigation patterns. RUM captures actual user journeys.

options.text: Anonymised mPulse RUM data — real user navigation patterns
options.explain: mPulse beacons capture what users actually click, enabling accurate next-page prediction.

options.text: Manual URL lists from the customer
options.explain: That's static prefetch — Predictive Prefetch is ML-driven and automatic.
It uses Akamai's own RUM data (mPulse), not third-party analytics.

quiz.question: Does Predictive Prefetch require origin changes?

options.text: Yes — you need a new API endpoint
options.explain: No origin changes. Configuration is entirely in Property Manager.

options.text: No — it's configured in Property Manager with no origin changes
options.explain: Enable in the rule tree, and the CDN handles prediction and prefetching at the edge.

options.text: Yes — you need to implement a recommendation engine
options.explain: Akamai's ML models handle prediction. No customer-side engine needed.
Framework-agnostic. Works with any web stack.
